methodology: Static source scan of all Math.random() calls in src/services/ and src/data/; classified by co-op shared-state impact

recentlyFixed.context: Enemy pool selection block — now uses 'enemyPool' seeded fork
assignEnemyIds — local rng param threaded through pickCombatEnemy/getMiniBossForFloor/weightedEnemyPick

remainingExposure.reason: Both co-op clients independently roll crit; one may crit and the other not. This produces different damage dealt to the shared enemy even within the same turn, potentially causing delta divergence after reconciliation.
Same as crit_lens — independently rolled per client. The two delta computations sent to the barrier will have different damage values depending on the roll outcome.

remainingExposure.context: Relic reward acquisition rarity roll and candidate pick
remainingExposure.reason: If co-op has a shared reward screen (architecture doc suggests it may), both clients independently pick a relic from the pool. They could surface different rarities and different relics to the player.

remainingExposure.context: Shop relic selection (rarity and candidate pick)
remainingExposure.reason: In solo/race mode this is per-player. If co-op ever shares a shop, this would desync. Currently co-op mode does not appear to use a shared shop, so impact is deferred.

remainingExposure.context: Fisher-Yates shuffle — used in deck manager for card ordering
remainingExposure.reason: Card draw order divergence between peers. In co-op the deck is per-player so this does not affect shared state directly, but replay determinism would be broken.
Quiz answer options are per-player UI, not shared game state. No desync risk for shared enemy HP.
